NestedIterator::NestedIterator(vector<NestedInteger> &nestedList) {

solution:
```cpp
#include <vector>
#include <queue>
using namespace std;

class NestedInteger {
public:
    // Existing interface methods as per the problem statement
    bool isInteger() const;
    int getInteger() const;
    const vector<NestedInteger> &getList() const;
};

class NestedIterator {
private:
    queue<int> resultQueue;
    
    void helper(const NestedInteger& ni) {
        if (ni.isInteger()) {
            resultQueue.push(ni.getInteger());
        } else {
            const vector<NestedInteger>& list = ni.getList();
            for (const auto& elem : list) {
                helper(elem);
            }
        }
    }

public:
    NestedIterator(vector<NestedInteger> &nestedList) {
        for (const auto& elem : nestedList) {
            helper(elem);
        }
    }
    
    int next() {
        int val = resultQueue.front();
        resultQueue.pop();
        return val;
    }
    
    bool hasNext() {
        return !resultQueue.empty();
    }
};
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a depth-first approach with a helper function to recursively flatten the nested list into a queue of integers. This ensures that the integers are processed in the correct order and allows the iterator methods to operate efficiently.
